examples.title: Dynamic Array Stack Implementation
examples.description: Implementation of a stack using dynamic array with push, pop, and resize functionality
examples.explanation: This code implements a stack using a dynamic array. When the array fills up (size equals capacity), the resize function doubles the capacity using realloc. The pop operation checks if the stack is too empty and can shrink the array to save memory.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) where n is number of elements

examples.title: Dynamic Array Class in Python
examples.description: Demonstrates dynamic array behavior with append operation and resize mechanism
examples.explanation: Python's list uses dynamic arrays internally. This custom implementation shows how append works: it doubles capacity when full. The resize creates a new larger array and copies all existing elements, which is O(n) but happens infrequently.
examples.timeComplexity: Append: O(1) amortized, Access by index: O(1)

examples.title: Dynamic Queue using Circular Array
examples.description: Implementation of queue using circular dynamic array with front and rear pointers
examples.explanation: This queue uses circular array indexing with modulo operations to wrap around. The resize function grows or shrinks the array as needed, copying elements in proper order. Both enqueue and dequeue are O(1) amortized.
examples.timeComplexity: Enqueue: O(1) amortized, Dequeue: O(1) amortized

examples.title: Generic Dynamic Array Algorithm
examples.description: Pseudocode showing core dynamic array operations with growth and shrink decisions
examples.code:
CLASS DynamicArray<T>
    DATA array: POINTER TO T
    DATA size: INTEGER
    DATA capacity: INTEGER
    DATA growthFactor: REAL := 2.0
    DATA shrinkThreshold: REAL := 0.25
    
    CONSTRUCTOR(initialCapacity: INTEGER)
        capacity ← initialCapacity
        size ← 0
        array ← ALLOCATE(capacity * SIZE OF T)
    
    PROCEDURE resize(newCapacity: INTEGER)
        newArray ← ALLOCATE(newCapacity * SIZE OF T)
        FOR i FROM 0 TO size-1 DO
            newArray[i] ← array[i]
        END FOR
        DEALLOCATE(array)
        array ← newArray
        capacity ← newCapacity
    
    PROCEDURE append(item: T)
        IF size = capacity THEN
            resize(INT(growthFactor * capacity))
        END IF
        array[size] ← item
        size ← size + 1
    
    PROCEDURE removeLast(): T
        IF size = 0 THEN
            RETURN ERROR "Array is empty"
        END IF
        item ← array[size - 1]
        size ← size - 1
        
        IF size < shrinkThreshold * capacity AND capacity > 1 THEN
            resize(MAX(1, INT(capacity / growthFactor)))
        END IF
        RETURN item
    
    FUNCTION get(index: INTEGER): T
        IF index < 0 OR index >= size THEN
            RETURN ERROR "Index out of bounds"
        END IF
        RETURN array[index]
    
    FUNCTION length(): INTEGER
        RETURN size
    
    FUNCTION getCapacity(): INTEGER
        RETURN capacity
    
    FUNCTION getLoadFactor(): REAL
        RETURN size / capacity
END CLASS
examples.explanation: This pseudocode shows a complete dynamic array implementation with key features: growth factor controls doubling, shrink threshold prevents memory waste, and resize handles both growing and shrinking. The append and removeLast operations maintain amortized O(1) complexity.
examples.timeComplexity: Append: O(1) amortized, RemoveLast: O(1) amortized, Get: O(1)
